blockingIssues.description: v2 kernel documentation claims 'Reduced __syncthreads() from 2 to 1' but the actual code has 2 __syncthreads() calls per variant (TP4 and TP2), identical to v1. The claimed 50% reduction in synchronization barriers was NOT implemented.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Either (1) Correct all documentation/comments to reflect actual syncthreads count, OR (2) Actually implement the single-barrier optimization by restructuring the cross-warp reduction to use a single syncthreads.

blockingIssues.description: v2 kernel has never been deployed or benchmarked on the dev server. All performance targets (<=50us vs baseline ~79us) are unverified.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Deploy to dev server and run tests/bench_allreduce_micro.py to measure actual latency improvement.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add remote deployment guidance to gpu-kernel-worker skill. When the worker environment lacks GPU access, provide explicit steps for: (1) Creating deployment scripts that run on dev server, (2) Marking tests as 'pending validation' in handoff, (3) Recording this as a justified deviation from TDD.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m2-profile-allreduce worker correctly identified they couldn't follow TDD procedure due to lack of GPU access, but had no guidance on how to proceed. The skill's 'Write Tests First (RED)' and 'Run tests to confirm they FAIL' steps cannot be followed without GPU access.

m2-profile-allreduce.keyFinding: v2 kernel documentation claims reduced syncthreads but code has same count as v1. Performance unverified on dev server.

keyFindings: Aggressive pipelining already implemented via double-buffer + stream overlap
